description: General pre-packaged food: mandatory labelling requirements under FIC regulation

pipeline_inputs.query_text: food labelling mandatory information allergens nutrition declaration requirements

ground_truth_requirements.description: Mandatory food information list: name of food, ingredients list, allergens, net quantity, date of minimum durability, storage conditions, name and address of operator, country of origin, instructions for use, nutrition declaration
Allergens listed in Annex II must be emphasised in the ingredients list through a typographical means clearly distinguishing them from the rest
Mandatory nutrition declaration must include energy value, fat, saturates, carbohydrate, sugars, protein, and salt
Food information shall not be misleading as to the characteristics, composition, nature, method of production, or properties of the food
Mandatory food information must be available and easily accessible for all foods delivered to the final consumer or mass caterers
Mandatory food information must appear in a minimum font size (1.2mm x-height) and be legible, indelible, and not hidden or obscured
Country of origin or place of provenance is mandatory where failure to indicate could mislead the consumer
ground_truth_requirements.notes: Art 24 defines terms; Art 26 has the obligation

ground_truth_requirements.description: The name of the food shall be its legal name, or in the absence thereof, its customary name or a descriptive name
The quantity of an ingredient or category of ingredients (QUID) must be indicated when it appears in the name, is emphasised, or is essential to characterise the food
Any voluntary food information provided on labels must comply with the mandatory labelling requirements and must not be misleading, ambiguous, or confusing

For packaging with largest surface area less than 10 cm², only food name, allergens, net quantity, and date of minimum durability are required; nutrition declaration is not required for foods listed in Annex V
The regulation applies to all food business operators at all stages of the food chain where their activities concern providing food information to consumers
The labelling, advertising and presentation of food must not mislead consumers as to characteristics, composition, nature, or properties
Food must not be placed on the market if it is unsafe (injurious to health or unfit for human consumption)
Food additives not sold to the final consumer must be labelled with name/E-number and relevant information
Foods containing food colours listed in Annex V must include an additional warning notice on the label
When a nutrition or health claim is made, mandatory nutrition labelling must be provided including specific nutrients relevant to the claim
Foods containing or produced from GMOs must be labelled with GMO-related information
